---
title: "Source · HIrisPlex-S (reference SNP panel)"
subtitle: "The 41-SNP forensic eye/hair/skin colour predictor (Chaitanya et al. 2018)"
---

## Why this source

HIrisPlex-S (Chaitanya et al. 2018, *Forensic Science International: Genetics*) is the standard
forensic DNA system for predicting **eye, hair, and skin** colour — 41 SNPs across two multiplex
assays (24 eye/hair + 17 skin). It is a curated **predictive panel, not a GWAS**: it carries no
per-SNP effect sizes here, so these rows are `needs_sumstats = True`. It's included as a
**reference set** so you can ask *"which of the canonical prediction loci does this compendium
cover?"* — a face-validity check for the atlas and a bridge toward scoring. rsID is the join key;
for a SNP shared with the atlas, the GWAS row is kept and `+hirisplex_s` is recorded in `source`.

```{python}
import sys
sys.path.insert(0, "scripts")
import pandas as pd
from harmonize import to_harmonized, const

hps = pd.read_csv("data/sources/hirisplex_s_snps.tsv", sep="\t",
                  dtype=str, keep_default_na=False)

mapping = {
    "rsid":        "rsid",
    "effect_type": const("unknown"),
    "trait":       lambda df: "HIrisPlex-S (" + df["panel"] + ")",
    "mapped_gene": "gene",
}
ref = to_harmonized(hps, mapping, source="hirisplex_s",
                    source_version="Chaitanya2018", needs_sumstats=True)
ref.to_csv("output/sources/hirisplex_s_harmonized.csv", index=False)
print(f"{len(ref)} HIrisPlex-S SNPs "
      f"({(hps['panel'] == 'eye/hair').sum()} eye/hair + {(hps['panel'] == 'skin').sum()} skin) "
      f"-> output/sources/hirisplex_s_harmonized.csv")
```

## How much of HIrisPlex-S does the compendium already cover?

Compared against the catalog pull (Output A) plus the Crawford source — the GWAS-derived loci.

```{python}
import glob

captured = set(pd.read_csv("output/catalog/pigmentation_gwas_catalog.csv",
                           dtype=str, keep_default_na=False)["rsid"])
for f in glob.glob("output/sources/*_harmonized.csv"):
    if "hirisplex" in f:
        continue
    captured |= set(pd.read_csv(f, dtype=str, keep_default_na=False)["rsid"])

hps = hps.copy()
hps["in_atlas"] = hps["rsid"].isin(captured)
n_in = int(hps["in_atlas"].sum())
print(f"{n_in} / {len(hps)} HIrisPlex-S SNPs are already in the pigment-gwas atlas")
missing = hps[~hps["in_atlas"]]
if len(missing):
    print("\nnot captured (added to the compendium as reference-only rows):")
    print(missing[["rsid", "gene", "panel"]].to_string(index=False))
```

## Coverage by panel

```{python}
import plotly.graph_objects as go

ct = hps.groupby(["panel", "in_atlas"]).size().unstack(fill_value=0)
panels = list(ct.index)
fig = go.Figure()
if True in ct.columns:
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(y=panels, x=ct[True], name="in atlas", orientation="h",
                         marker_color="#55A868",
                         hovertemplate="%{y}: %{x} in atlas<extra></extra>"))
if False in ct.columns:
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(y=panels, x=ct[False], name="not captured", orientation="h",
                         marker_color="#C44E52",
                         hovertemplate="%{y}: %{x} not captured<extra></extra>"))
fig.update_layout(
    barmode="stack",
    title=f"HIrisPlex-S coverage by the pigment-gwas atlas — {n_in}/{len(hps)} SNPs",
    xaxis_title="SNPs", yaxis=dict(title="", autorange="reversed"),
    template="simple_white", height=260, legend_title_text="",
    margin=dict(t=56, l=80, r=16, b=40))
fig
```

*The 41 SNPs are transcribed from Chaitanya et al. 2018 (the two multiplex assays: 24 eye/hair +
17 skin). This is a membership/reference panel — no effect sizes — so every row keeps
`needs_sumstats = True` and does not compete on precedence with the GWAS sources.*
